prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Lo, in the quarter of the third, the lords of the SaaS realm didst summon unto themselves a great revenue, yet none couldst explain whence these coinless treasures appeared, for all commerce was transacted in the ether. I beheld strange scrolls branded with the sigil of “monthly active users,” though not a single serf nor peasant was seen laboring in the meadows. A council of wizards, known in whispers as “Developers,” gathered weekly and cast innumerable spells called Updates, wherein bugs were both slain and oft reborn. To mine everlasting confusion, the townsfolk rejoiced mightily at the advent of “feature parity,” though not a single jousting tournament or harvest feast was held in its honor.
